name: Triage inbound requests with Zendesk and Twilio without manual work

parameters.errorMessage: Missing required field 'subject' on incoming ticket.

parameters.message: =✅ Triage inbound requests with Zendesk and Twilio without manual work — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09985-ticket`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.subject }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Route by Work Type · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: bug, billing, howto + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'bug' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'billing' + Alert · ③→ Fast-Track 'howto' + Alert · ④→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'bug' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Ticket Tracker', matched on `ticketId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'billing' + Alert

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Fast-Track 'howto' + Alert

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Appends a new row to 'Ticket Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Sort Tickets Newest First

### Sort Tickets Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Guard Non-Empty subject

### Guard Non-Empty subject

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Ticket Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Twilio · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Twilio

### Escalate Failure to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

# Triage inbound requests with Zendesk and Twilio without manual work

**Pattern:** Ticket Triage · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Triage inbound requests with Zendesk and Twilio without manual work. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual feedback is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Required Fields Present? → Route by Work Type → Process 'bug' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Sort Tickets Newest First → Guard Non-Empty subject → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ticket triage without manual effort
- Anyone automating feedback in customer support
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your feedback process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
